rule.statement: Verify that each target widget has the core implementation files expected by the project packaging flow
rule.details: At minimum, inspect the widget implementation structure and confirm that the widget is not obviously incomplete before attempting packaging.

rule.statement: Check naming and folder consistency for the target widget
rule.details: When a widget diverges from the dominant project layout, report it clearly because it may affect packaging.

rule.statement: Validate JSON-based metadata files when they are part of the packaging path
rule.details: This includes config files and business object definitions used by the selected widget set.

rule.statement: Ensure the selected widget list matches the user request
Confirm toolkit configuration changes are intentional and limited to the packaging goal
Verify that the expected TWX artifact is produced at the expected location
Summarize packaging warnings separately from blocking failures
Report the output artifact path in a user-actionable way
Only proceed with deployment steps when the user explicitly requests deployment
If deployment is asynchronous, track and report the deployment status clearly
